source_document: FMCSA Dataset Description and Data Definitions - Select Datasets on DOT's Open Data Catalog

dataset_variants.name: InsHist
dataset_variants.description: Includes records that have been updated or added since the previous run of the dataset.

dataset_variants.name: InsHist - All With History
dataset_variants.description: Includes all records (including historical values where appropriate) as of the latest dataset update.

update_frequency: Daily by 9:30 AM US Eastern Time
dataset_description: Contains information on a carrier's/broker's/freight forwarder's previous insurance policy(ies). Includes the DOT number and docket number of the entity, the cancellation method (cancelled, replaced, name change, transferred), the type of policy, the policy number, and the effective and cancellation dates of the policy.
important_note: All insurance information is related to the insurance policy either being cancelled, being replaced, or prior to a name change. It is NOT the subsequent (if applicable) policy.

fields.definition: Unique FMCSA number identifying for-hire motor carriers (MC000000, FF000000 or MX000000)
Official FMCSA registration number for all interstate motor carriers

fields.note: For Form Codes 91, 91X, and 82, the insurance amounts are contained in fields #12 and #13. For Form Codes 34, 83, 84, and 85, the amounts displayed in #12 and #13 will be 0, as they are not BI&PD policies.

fields.definition: The method by which the policy ended

values.replaced: Policy was replaced by a new policy

fields.definition: The form that either executed the cancellation, replaced the form indicated in field #3, or executed the name change/transfer
fields.note: Codes for 'Replaced' will be one of the form codes indicated in field #3 (34, 82, 83, 84, 85, 91, 91X)

fields.definition: Indicates whether the policy is BI&PD or another type

values.*: Not BIPD (Cargo, Surety, or Trust Fund)

fields.definition: Description of insurance form/class (corresponds to Form Code in field #3)
The minimum insurance amount required for the entity (carrier, broker, etc.), in thousands
For BI&PD form codes; indicates whether the insurance policy is primary or excess
Effective date of the insurance policy
Amount (in thousands). When the insurance class code (#10) is 'E', the underlying limit amount is the value of the primary insurance.
fields.note: Only populated for Form Codes 91, 91X, and 82. Will be 0 for Form Codes 34, 83, 84, and 85.

fields.definition: The maximum dollar amount covered by the insurance policy (in thousands)
fields.note: Only populated for Form Codes 91, 91X, and 82. Will be 0 for Form Codes 34, 83, 84, and 85.

fields.definition: Date the policy is effectively cancelled

values.Term/REPL: Replacement was executed by a new policy submission

content_disclaimer: Data is provided as a public service by FMCSA for informational purposes only. It is a snapshot at the time of generation, is constantly changing, and does not constitute legal advice or a legal contract.
